title: Micranthemum multiflorum
intro: Micranthemum multiflorum, also known as multiflorum, is a plant species in the family Plantaginaceae, characterized by its delicate white flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Micranthemum multiflorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Micranthemum multiflorum is often prepared as a tea or tincture, as well as being used in infusions and salves, to harness its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Micranthemum multiflorum is classified under the family Primulaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as primulas. This family consists of over 1,000 species, with Micranthemum multiflorum being one of the many species found within it.
